justification: Brief is clearly scoped to Sprint 1. Sprint 2 retry logic is explicitly acknowledged and excluded with direct instruction to Shaq ('do NOT implement retry logic, job queues, or backoff'). All three original ACs are present (success, non-2xx failure, timeout failure) plus a bonus POST/JSON AC. 5-second timeout is included as both a domain rule and architecture guidance with named constant. MJ's 'leave extension points' guidance is passed through as a design note in architecture_guidance, pragmatically scoped to returning DeliveryResult rather than over-engineering hooks. No retry logic appears in implementation guidance.

observations.text: Sprint 2 deferral is stated in task_context AND reinforced in architecture_guidance with explicit 'Do NOT implement retry, backoff, job queues, or BullMQ'
All three ACs present: successful delivery, non-2xx failure, timeout failure
5-second timeout treated as a domain rule with named constant recommendation (DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_MS = 5000)
Extension points guidance passed through pragmatically — DeliveryResult enables future retry layering without premature abstractions
Added a fourth AC for POST with JSON body, going beyond minimum requirements
Terminology alignment section adds clarity for Shaq on domain-to-code mapping
Open questions about network errors and timeout http_status_code show appropriate uncertainty without overstepping scope
